spec.title: Headless text render and audio sink contracts

humanSummary.text: UTSUSHI-022 Slice A delivers the engine-neutral, headless sink substrate as specified. The new `utsushi_core::sink` module exposes `SinkKind`, `SinkCapability`, `SinkSet`, `SinkCapabilitySummary`, `SinkError`/`SinkResult`/`codes`, and the three trait surfaces `TextSurfaceSink`/`FrameArtifactSink`/`AudioEventSink` plus their payload types `TextLine`/`FrameArtifact`/`AudioEvent` (and `AudioEventKind`). All three traits are `Send + Sync` with `&self`-only methods (no `&mut self` anywhere under `crates/utsushi-core/src/sink/`), interior mutability is left to implementors, and no trait surface or payload field uses `std::path::Path` or `PathBuf` — path-shaped fields use the UTSUSHI-020 `AssetId` (now with a serialize-as-`vfs://`-string serde impl that round-trips through `AssetId::parse`) or `ObservationArtifactRef`. Engine-neutrality is preserved: an exhaustive grep across the sink module for `xp3|kirikiri|kag|rgss|rpgmaker` returned zero matches outside two false positives in doc comments (the substring `kag` appears only inside `linkage`). The per-sink ceilings are pinned by `SinkKind::evidence_tier_ceiling`: TextSurface→E1, AudioEvent→E0, FrameArtifact→E4 (intentional change from the plan §3.1 prose). The plan §5 table is authoritative and explicitly states `Enforced ceiling: E4 (only for ReferenceFidelity)` with an `E2 minimum required` lower bound for frame artifacts; that floor is enforced by `FrameArtifact::validate` (`evidence_tier < EvidenceTier::E2 → SinkError::EvidenceTierMismatch` with `ceiling: E2`) and is covered by `frame_sink_rejects_e1_emission_as_evidence_tier_mismatch` and `frame_sink_rejects_e0_emission_as_evidence_tier_mismatch`. The descriptor-side guard that an E1-ceiling adapter cannot register a Supported-E2 `FrameArtifactSink` is correctly deferred (plan §5 last paragraph) and not present in this slice — captured as F002 below. Frame artifact refs are policy-gated: `FrameArtifact::validate` calls `ObservationArtifactRef::validate` (which routes through `validate_runtime_artifact_uri` rejecting absolute paths, file://, blob://, data://, traversal, and non-managed roots) and an `FRAME_ARTIFACT_KIND_ALLOW_LIST` of `screenshot|frame_capture|recording`. Bytes never enter the sink — only refs do; the integration test in `crates/utsushi-core/tests/sink_contracts.rs` writes bytes through `RuntimeArtifactRoot::write_bytes` and only then emits the announcement. `AudioEvent` has no `bytes`/`sampleRate`/`duration`/`mixLevel`/`channels` field (structural test `audio_sink_payload_has_no_audio_bytes_field` enforces this) and `AudioEvent::validate` rejects any tier `!= E0`. `SinkError::UnsupportedKind` is the documented non-silent diagnostic; all three sink tests exercise the capability-`Unsupported` adapter path and `codes::ALL` mirrors the UTSUSHI-020 `vfs::diagnostics::codes::ALL` precedent, so a downstream allowed-code validator cannot drift. The `BudgetExhausted` variant is unreachable from any production code path — `inject_budget_exhaustion` is declared inside `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` in `audio.rs` and the corresponding `budget_inject: Mutex<Option<String>>` field lives only on the in-test collector struct, never on the production sink contract. UTSUSHI-029 will wire the production hook through `RuntimeArtifactRoot::write_bytes`, tracked as F001. `RuntimeRequest` extension is strictly additive: one new `pub sinks: Option<SinkSet>` field, one new `with_sinks` builder, one new Debug field (`<present>`/`<absent>` marker, no implementor type leakage — exercised by `runtime_request_debug_does_not_expose_sink_implementor_type`), and `RuntimeRequest::new` continues to default `sinks: None`. `utsushi-fixture` is untouched (`git diff main..HEAD -- crates/utsushi-fixture/` returns empty). The new `utsushi_core::redaction::reject_unredacted_local_paths` re-export is a thin pub wrapper around the existing crate-private helper and is exercised by the cross-sink integration test, which serializes one `TextLine`, one `FrameArtifact`, and one `AudioEvent` and asserts the combined JSON passes the filter; the negative test in `text.rs` confirms the wrapper catches a `/home/...` substring planted in `speaker`. The new `Sink Tier Rules` section in `docs/utsushi-fidelity-policy.md` is placed between `Adapter Capability Contract` and `Runtime Environment Matrix` as planned, restates the per-sink ceilings, lists the four `utsushi.sink.*` codes that surface on violation, and explicitly forbids promoting `unsupported_kind` to higher tiers on the basis of empty event lists. Verification re-runs from the worktree: `cargo test -p utsushi-core` passes 128 lib + 3 `sink_contracts.rs` + 16 `vfs_synthetic_package.rs` (147 total; 5 ignored), `cargo test -p utsushi-fixture` passes 22, `cargo clippy -p utsushi-core --all-targets -- -D warnings` is clean, `cargo fmt --check` is clean, `just schema` passes 124 schema tests, and `just check` exits 0 across the workspace. Outcome: follow_up_only with two non-blocking P2 follow-ups (BudgetExhausted production wire-in, descriptor-vs-capability cross-check) and one P3 follow-up (voice-subtitle-sync taxonomy variant when the first port needs it).

findings.title: Wire SinkError::BudgetExhausted into RuntimeArtifactRoot::write_bytes once UTSUSHI-029 lands
findings.description: The `SinkError::BudgetExhausted { sink, budget }` variant is defined, carries the stable `utsushi.sink.budget_exhausted` semantic code, and is exercised by `budget_exhausted_variant_is_reachable_via_injection_point_but_not_wired_in_production` in `sink/audio.rs`. However, the injection point (`inject_budget_exhaustion` plus the `budget_inject: Mutex<Option<String>>` field) lives inside the `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` block, so the variant is only reachable from test code. The production sink path never returns it. The plan and the new `Soft budget exhaustion` paragraph in `docs/utsushi-fidelity-policy.md` both explicitly defer the production hook to UTSUSHI-029 (`RuntimeArtifactRoot::write_bytes` routing the diagnostic when an artifact-budget tracker is registered). This is acceptable for Slice A — the ABI is forward-compatible — but the production wire-up must land with UTSUSHI-029 so the `Artifact Limits` table in the fidelity policy has a real enforcement point.

evidence.detail: `crates/utsushi-core/src/sink/audio.rs` declares `inject_budget_exhaustion` under `#[cfg(test)]` and stores `budget_inject` only on the in-test `CollectingAudioSink`, not on any production trait surface.
`docs/utsushi-fidelity-policy.md` §Sink Tier Rules / Soft budget exhaustion explicitly defers the wire-up to UTSUSHI-029 and `RuntimeArtifactRoot::write_bytes`.
Test `budget_exhausted_variant_is_reachable_via_injection_point_but_not_wired_in_production` confirms the variant is constructable but not reachable from a real adapter path.

findings.impact: Without a production hook, soft artifact-budget enforcement remains best-effort. The diagnostic surface and semantic code are stable, so the wire-up is additive and will not break existing adapters; the consequence of delay is only that the fidelity policy's `artifactLimits` cannot be enforced until UTSUSHI-029 lands.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: `RuntimeArtifactRoot::write_bytes` (or a budget tracker it delegates to) returns `SinkError::BudgetExhausted { sink: SinkKind::FrameArtifact, budget }` when a configured soft budget is exceeded.
A non-test integration test demonstrates a real adapter receiving `SinkError::BudgetExhausted` when its artifact stream exceeds a configured byte/count cap.
Test-only `inject_budget_exhaustion` helpers can be removed or kept only as adversarial fuzz inputs; they must not be the only path to the variant.

proposedDagNode.title: Wire SinkError::BudgetExhausted into the runtime artifact root budget hook
proposedDagNode.summary: Route the soft artifact-budget violation surface from RuntimeArtifactRoot::write_bytes (or its delegate budget tracker) to SinkError::BudgetExhausted so the diagnostic is reachable from a real adapter path rather than only the cfg(test) injection point.
deliverables: Production budget tracker on RuntimeArtifactRoot::write_bytes returning utsushi.sink.budget_exhausted
Adapter-side propagation so FrameArtifactSink::emit_frame surfaces BudgetExhausted instead of ArtifactPolicy when bytes exceed the cap
Removal or repurposing of the cfg(test) inject_budget_exhaustion helper
acceptanceCriteria: A real adapter writing more bytes than the configured soft budget receives SinkError::BudgetExhausted with the correct sink and budget label.
The semantic code utsushi.sink.budget_exhausted is reachable without using cfg(test) helpers.
The fidelity policy artifactLimits table cites the wire-up location and the test that exercises it.
auditFocus: No silent drop of budget-exhausted emissions
Soft budget enforcement matches docs/utsushi-fidelity-policy.md artifactLimits

findings.title: Cross-check that an E1-ceiling adapter cannot register a Supported-E2 FrameArtifactSink
findings.description: The plan §5 last paragraph commits to a runtime-time assertion that an adapter declaring `evidence_tier_ceiling = E1` does not register a `FrameArtifactSink` with capability `Supported { evidence_tier_ceiling: E2 }`. Slice A intentionally keeps `RuntimeAdapterDescriptor` structurally unchanged and exposes a typed view through `SinkSet::capabilities()` so conformance (UTSUSHI-026) can inspect later. The follow-up is to implement that conformance check — either inside `SinkSet::capabilities()` (returning a diagnostic on the cross-check fail) or as a descriptor-side validator that runs at adapter registration. Without it, a misconfigured adapter could declare a text-only ceiling at the descriptor level and still expose an E2 frame sink, and the per-sink ceiling alone would not catch the inconsistency (the frame sink's own ceiling allows up to E4).

evidence.detail: Plan `.plan/UTSUSHI-022.md` §5: 'a follow-up audit asserts that an adapter declaring `evidence_tier_ceiling = E1` does not register a `FrameArtifactSink` with capability `Supported { evidence_tier_ceiling: E2 }`. That check is added as a separate runtime-time assertion in `SinkSet::capabilities()`'
`SinkSet::capabilities` currently produces a passive snapshot; it does not cross-check the adapter descriptor's overall evidence_tier_ceiling.

findings.impact: An adapter could mis-declare evidence ceilings (descriptor-level E1, frame-sink E2) and the substrate would not catch it. Conformance reports for capture/recording (UTSUSHI-029) could then accept E2 captures from an adapter whose top-level ceiling forbids them. The slice ships honestly because the deferral is documented; the follow-up closes the structural loophole.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: A descriptor or SinkSet validator returns a typed diagnostic when an adapter's overall evidence_tier_ceiling is below the per-sink Supported ceiling declared by any of its registered sinks.
A unit test demonstrates an E1-ceiling adapter registering a Supported E2 frame sink and asserts the diagnostic surfaces with a stable semantic code.
The fidelity policy section documents the cross-check rule alongside the existing per-sink ceiling rules.

proposedDagNode.title: Descriptor and SinkSet evidence-tier cross-check
proposedDagNode.summary: Add the planned runtime-time cross-check that prevents an adapter with an E1 overall evidence_tier_ceiling from registering a Supported-E2 FrameArtifactSink, closing the structural loophole the per-sink ceiling alone does not cover.
deliverables: Cross-check validator (descriptor- or SinkSet-side) that compares the adapter's overall evidence_tier_ceiling against each registered sink's Supported ceiling
Stable semantic code under utsushi.sink.* (e.g. utsushi.sink.descriptor_ceiling_below_sink) wired through SinkError or a sibling diagnostic enum
Test coverage including the E1-adapter / E2-frame-sink mismatch case and the matching-ceiling baseline
acceptanceCriteria: An adapter whose overall evidence_tier_ceiling is below any registered sink's Supported ceiling fails registration or surfaces a typed diagnostic at SinkSet::capabilities-time.
The conformance manifest from UTSUSHI-026 references the cross-check rule.
All existing tests in utsushi-core continue to pass without modification.
auditFocus: No silent acceptance of over-claimed sink ceilings

findings.title: Voice-subtitle sync AudioEventKind variant deferred until a port exercises it
findings.description: `AudioEventKind` covers `BgmStart`, `BgmStop`, `SeFire`, `VoicePlay`, `VoiceStop`, and `Marker`. The plan §11.3 risks section flags voice-subtitle sync as an unknown: whether it needs its own variant or fits inside `VoicePlay`/`Marker` plus the `bridge_ref` linkage. The current shape can express a voice line tied to a bridge unit (the integration test does exactly this with `bridge_ref` populated on a `VoicePlay`), but a dedicated `VoiceSubtitleSync` variant would let downstream conformance distinguish the start of a voiced line from a metadata marker tied to that line. Adding the variant is a small additive enum extension; the audit recommends deferring until the first runtime port that exercises it (likely an MV/MZ or RealLive voice fixture) requests the distinction.

evidence.detail: Plan `.plan/UTSUSHI-022.md` §11.3 explicitly defers the voice-subtitle sync taxonomy decision to the first port that needs it.
`audio_sink_accepts_e0_voice_play_with_bridge_ref` in `sink/audio.rs` already exercises the bridge-ref-on-VoicePlay shape that the deferred variant would refine.

findings.impact: Low. Adapters can already announce voiced lines via `VoicePlay` plus `bridge_ref`. The lack of a dedicated variant only matters if downstream conformance needs to distinguish 'voice playback started' from 'voice tied to subtitle' inside the same emission stream; today no consumer requires this.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: The first runtime port that emits voiced dialogue tied to a subtitle drives a decision (either documenting that VoicePlay+bridge_ref is sufficient or adding the new AudioEventKind variant).
If a new variant is added, the snake_case serde mapping, `as_str` arm, and at least one acceptance test are added together.
The fidelity policy audio section reflects the decision.

proposedDagNode.title: Voice-subtitle sync AudioEventKind taxonomy extension
proposedDagNode.summary: Extend AudioEventKind with the voice-subtitle sync shape (or document that VoicePlay+bridge_ref already covers it) once the first runtime port emits voiced dialogue tied to a subtitle bridge unit.
deliverables: AudioEventKind taxonomy update (either a new variant with snake_case serde mapping plus as_str arm, or an inline doc-comment recording that VoicePlay+bridge_ref is sufficient)
Acceptance test exercising the chosen shape against a voiced-line bridge unit
Fidelity policy audio section updated to reflect the taxonomy outcome
acceptanceCriteria: Downstream conformance can distinguish a voiced line tied to a subtitle from a generic VoicePlay marker (either via the existing bridge_ref shape or via a new variant).
All existing audio sink tests continue to pass.
No engine-specific opcode or shape leaks into the taxonomy.
auditFocus: No engine opcode leakage into AudioEventKind
